seoTitle: How to Set Up a Zendesk Help Center From Scratch
metaDescription: Learn how to set up a Zendesk Help Center from the ground up, covering language settings, category structure, branding, and going live.
intro: Without an organized self-service portal, agents end up answering the same repetitive questions over and over. This guide walks through the full setup of a new Zendesk Help Center, from activating Guide to structuring categories and publishing your first live article.

steps.heading: Activate the Guide setup
steps.body: From the top product matrix icon, click Knowledge and select Get Started to enter the setup environment.

steps.heading: Set your default language
steps.body: Configure Guide Admin language parameters and lock in your default workspace locale before drafting content.

steps.heading: Build your category structure
steps.body: Go to Arrange Content and click Add to build your initial Categories and nested Sections.

steps.heading: Apply branding
steps.body: Open the Customize Design panel to upload your logo and adjust visual theme colors.

steps.heading: Publish a baseline article
steps.body: Draft and publish at least one support article inside your new section so the portal isn't empty.

steps.heading: Go live
steps.body: Open the left sidebar settings gear, scroll to Help Center, and click Activate Live to move from a restricted preview to a public portal.

faq.question: Do I need to publish content before going live?
faq.answer: It's recommended to publish at least one baseline article so visitors don't land on an empty portal.

faq.question: Can I change my default language later?
faq.answer: You can adjust locale settings later, but it's best to lock in your primary language before drafting a large volume of content.

faq.question: How is a category different from a section?
faq.answer: Categories are broad top-level groupings, while sections are more focused sub-groups nested inside a category.

faq.question: Can I preview the Help Center before making it public?
faq.answer: Yes, the portal stays in a restricted preview state until you explicitly activate it live.
